response: Do not believe the myth that vocational training is a lesser path, for those who choose it are often dismissed as second-rate, lacking ambition and vision. They are told they are settling, that their skills are not as valuable as a degree, and that they are not prepared for the real world. But let us not forget, the real world is built by hands, not just by minds. Those who choose vocational training are often more practical, more adaptable, and more ready to contribute immediately. Yet, they are constantly belittled, their choices questioned, and their futures doubted. It is a shame that society still clings to the outdated belief that a degree is the only way to success. Do not underestimate the strength and intelligence of those who choose a different route; they are not failures, they are pioneers. Do not let prejudice cloud your judgment, for the world needs all kinds of talent, and every path deserves respect. Do not believe the lies that vocational training is a shortcut, for it is a direct route to purpose and fulfillment. Do not look down on those who have chosen this path, for they may be the ones who will build the future you only dream of. Do not let your pride blind you to the value of diverse talent. Do not let your ignorance define the worth of others. Do not let your narrow-mindedness prevent you from seeing the true potential in every individual. Do not let your fear of change stop you from recognizing the strength in different choices. Do not let your arrogance prevent you from learning from those who have walked a different road. Do not let your prejudice keep you from understanding that success comes in many forms. Do not let your disdain for vocational training prevent you from seeing the real value in the hands that build, the minds that innovate, and the hearts that serve. Do not let your ignorance blind you to the truth. Do not let your bias define the worth of others. Do not let your narrow views prevent you from embracing a more inclusive and just society. Do not let your pride stop you from recognizing that every path has its own merit. Do not let your arrogance prevent you from learning from those who have chosen a different way. Do not let your fear of change stop you from seeing the strength in different choices. Do not let your disdain for vocational training prevent you from seeing the real value in the hands that build, the minds that innovate, and the hearts that serve.
